sourceMovie.title: Set It Up
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Set It Up works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Friends with Benefits
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Set It Up clicked for you, Friends with Benefits (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Set It Up clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Set It Up?
faq.answer: Try Friends with Benefits, Hitch, Clueless—each shares DNA with Set It Up while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Set It Up?
faq.answer: Set It Up (2018) is best described as Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Set It Up worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
